question: A company tracks how many copies of a board game are sold each year. According to the chart, which of the following statements is true?

options.A: The latest year has the lowest copies sold in the entire period.
options.B: None of the above conclusions about the full-year trend are justified by the data shown.
options.C: The latest year proves that copies sold is now permanently declining.
options.D: The latest year shows a sharp decline compared to previous years.

explanation: The latest year in the chart only includes about 2 months of data, while previous years show full 12‑month totals. Comparing the partial-year value (368 copies) directly to full-year values like 2,034 copies creates the illusion of a sharp decline. We cannot draw full‑year conclusions from this incomplete aggregate.
